title: Professional Phone Call: Inquiries and Messages
opening: Good morning, Sterling Solutions, Customer Relations. This is Alex speaking. How may I help you today?

t1.prompt: Good morning, Sterling Solutions, Customer Relations. This is Alex speaking. How may I help you today?
t1.promptTranslation: Good morning, Sterling Solutions, Customer Relations. This is Alex speaking. How may I help you today? — A common formal greeting in professional phone calls, identifying the company, department, and speaker, then politely offering assistance.

t2_ask_caller.prompt: Certainly. May I ask who's calling, please?
t2_ask_caller.promptTranslation: Certainly. May I ask who's calling, please? — A polite way to ascertain the caller's identity before connecting them or providing information. Note the formal 'May I ask'.

t2_ask_purpose.prompt: And who were you looking to speak with regarding that matter, or what's the nature of your enquiry?
t2_ask_purpose.promptTranslation: And who were you looking to speak with regarding that matter, or what's the nature of your enquiry? — This asks for the specific person or the topic of the call. 'Regarding that matter' (formal) and 'nature of your enquiry' (professional idiom for 'what is your question about').

t3_unavailable.prompt: I'm afraid Mr. Johnson is currently tied up in a critical meeting and isn't taking calls at the moment. Would you care to leave a message, or shall I have him return your call as soon as he's free?
t3_unavailable.promptTranslation: I'm afraid Mr. Johnson is currently tied up in a critical meeting and isn't taking calls at the moment. Would you care to leave a message, or shall I have him return your call as soon as he's free? — 'Tied up' (idiom: busy/unavailable) and 'return your call' (idiom: call back) are common polite expressions. 'Care to leave a message' is a polite offer.

t3_transfer_or_help.prompt: Understood. I can either put you through to Accounts, or perhaps I can assist you directly with a general query?
t3_transfer_or_help.promptTranslation: Understood. I can either put you through to Accounts, or perhaps I can assist you directly with a general query? — 'Put you through' means to connect the call. 'Assist you directly' means the speaker (Alex) will help. 'General query' is a broad question.

t4_take_message.prompt: Certainly. What's the message, and could you confirm your contact number for him?
t4_take_message.promptTranslation: Certainly. What's the message, and could you confirm your contact number for him? — A clear request for the content of the message and the caller's phone number, using 'confirm' for politeness.

t4_take_callback.prompt: Right. What would be the most convenient number and time for him to get back to you?
t4_take_callback.promptTranslation: Right. What would be the most convenient number and time for him to get back to you? — 'Convenient number and time' is a polite and professional way to ask for the best contact details for a return call.

t4_general_assistance.prompt: Of course. How can I assist you with your query?
t4_general_assistance.promptTranslation: Of course. How can I assist you with your query? — A straightforward offer of help, using 'query' as a professional term for a question.

t-repeat.prompt: I'm sorry, I didn't quite catch that. Could you please repeat what you said?
t-repeat.promptTranslation: I'm sorry, I didn't quite catch that. Could you please repeat what you said? — A standard, polite phrase used when the speaker did not hear or understand the previous statement.

t-end.prompt: Thank you for calling Sterling Solutions. Have a good day.
t-end.promptTranslation: Thank you for calling Sterling Solutions. Have a good day. — A standard professional closing remark for a business call.
